skill.name: mindmap
skill.description: Generate mindmaps locally/offline with native HTML/CSS/JS (no external libraries); use when you need to parse Plain Text node structures or map LLM outputs into an on-device visualization.
skill.summary: Convert plain text hierarchies into self-contained HTML mindmaps that render in any browser with zero external dependencies.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 45 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The skill is a documentation-only guide for generating offline mindmaps using native HTML/CSS/JS. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown backtick formatting as shell execution (21 occurrences), innocent technical terms as weak cryptographic algorithms (22 occurrences), and a source attribution URL as a network concern (2 occurrences). No executable scripts, network calls, or data processing logic exists in this skill. No prompt injection attempts or malicious patterns were detected.

content.user_title: Generate Mindmaps Offline with Plain Text
content.value_statement: Plain text hierarchies are difficult to visualize without external tools. This skill converts indented text into self-contained HTML mindmaps that render in any browser with no dependencies or network access.
actual_capabilities: Parse indented plain text into hierarchical mindmap node structures
Generate self-contained HTML files with native CSS and JS mindmap rendering
Render mindmaps in offline and air-gapped environments without CDN access
Support customizable indentation rules and node labeling parameters
Produce deterministic output files with validation summaries
Stream or incrementally update mindmap content from AI or LLM output
limitations: Does not support image nodes or rich media within mindmap nodes
Mindmap layout is limited to horizontal tree arrangement only
Does not support interactive editing or drag-and-drop node rearrangement
Large mindmaps with hundreds of nodes may have rendering performance constraints

use_cases.title: Visualize Research Literature Hierarchies
use_cases.description: Convert structured research notes into a visual mindmap for identifying relationships between topics, subtopics, and findings.

use_cases.title: Map Project Work Breakdown Structures
use_cases.description: Transform project task lists into visual work breakdown structures for stakeholder reviews and planning sessions.

use_cases.title: Outline Document and Article Structures
use_cases.description: Convert article or book outlines into visual mindmaps to verify structural completeness before writing.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Mindmap from an Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a mindmap from this outline: [paste indented text here]. Generate a self-contained HTML file that renders the mindmap in a browser.
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting a simple indented outline into a visual mindmap

prompt_templates.title: Customize Node Colors by Hierarchy Level
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a mindmap from this text with custom colors for each depth level: [paste text]. Use different background colors for level 1, 2, and 3 nodes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a color-coded mindmap for presentations or reports

prompt_templates.title: Stream Mindmap from Real-Time AI Output
prompt_templates.prompt: As you generate analysis results, format the output as indented plain text. Parse each section into the mindmap HTML template incrementally so the visualization updates as new content arrives.
prompt_templates.scenario: Real-time mindmap generation from streaming AI analysis

prompt_templates.title: Generate Mindmap with Automated Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse this hierarchical data into a mindmap HTML file. After generation, validate the output and include a summary showing node count, maximum depth, and any formatting issues detected.
prompt_templates.scenario: Production use with automated quality validation requirements

output_examples.input: Indented text hierarchy of a project plan with phases and tasks
output_examples.output: A self-contained HTML file opens in the browser showing a horizontal mindmap. The root node displays the project name. Phase nodes spread to the right with individual task leaf nodes beneath each. SVG connection lines link parent to child nodes with clear visual hierarchy. The file requires no internet connection.

output_examples.input: Research paper outline with title, sections, and subsections
output_examples.output: A browser-rendered mindmap with the paper title as the central node. Section headings branch to the right with subsections arranged vertically beneath each. Related subtopics cluster together using DFS ordering. The single HTML file contains all styling and logic inline.

best_practices: Use consistent indentation of exactly 2 spaces per level for reliable parsing
Keep node text to short phrases under 50 characters to avoid text overflow
Test the generated mindmap by opening the HTML file directly in a browser
anti_patterns: Do not use tab characters for indentation - the parser expects spaces only
Do not include empty lines within the hierarchy as they may break node continuity
Do not expect interactive drag-and-drop or real-time editing capabilities

faq.question: What tools do I need to use this mindmap skill?
faq.answer: You only need a modern web browser such as Chrome, Firefox, or Edge. No external libraries or packages are required.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access to work?
faq.answer: No. The generated HTML file uses only native HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with no external dependencies or CDN resources.

faq.question: What format should the input text follow?
faq.answer: The input uses plain text indentation with 2 spaces per level. Each line represents a node in the hierarchy.

faq.question: Can I customize the mindmap colors and styles?
faq.answer: Yes. The generated HTML contains inline CSS that you can edit directly to change colors, spacing, fonts, and node styles.

faq.question: What happens if the input text cannot be parsed?
faq.answer: The template falls back to displaying default example data and indicates that the input format was not recognized.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with very large mindmaps?
faq.answer: The skill works with any hierarchy size, but very large mindmaps with hundreds of nodes may render slowly due to the simple layout algorithm.
